subject: [RFC PATCH v4 11/27] mm/huge_memory.c: add private node folio split notification callback

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: Raised concerns about the implementation details, specifically the use of a wrapper function to consolidate zone device and private node callback dispatch. Suggested re-evaluating the approach to ensure it is efficient and scalable.

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Agreed with David's concerns and suggested exploring alternative solutions, such as using a more generic callback mechanism. Also asked for clarification on the specific use cases where private node services need to update internal metadata.

2026-02-22.patch_summary: This RFC patch proposes adding a private node folio split notification callback to the Linux kernel's huge memory management code. The goal is to allow private node services to update internal metadata when a Transparent Huge Page (THP) folio is split, similar to how zone device services already handle this scenario.
